method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — bklynlibrary.org/robots.txt is a standard Drupal robots.txt disallowing /core/, /admin/, /search/, /user/register etc.; nothing crawled was disallowed). Fingerprinted www.bklynlibrary.org as Drupal behind Cloudflare (drupal-settings-json, /core//profiles//modules//themes/ + composer scaffold paths, x-generator patterns). Discovered the Drupal JSON:API module is fully PUBLIC at www.bklynlibrary.org/jsonapi (289 resource links, ~22 node types) and enumerated node--branch/event/book_profile/eres/digital_asset field schemas from live records. Identified the catalog as BiblioCommons (bklynlibrary.bibliocommons.com, 302 to select_library, SRV/app cookies) and the events calendar as discover.bklynlibrary.org (PHP/Cloudflare). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Brooklyn Public Library (BPL)' via the Socrata Discovery API — only 2 assets, both type=href (external links, no SODA table).
headline_findings: BPL is an independent NONPROFIT (not a city agency) running Drupal behind Cloudflare, with the catalog on the BiblioCommons ILS and events on discover.bklynlibrary.org.

The standout finding is the inverse of the usual one: BPL's content data is ALREADY a working public API. The Drupal JSON:API module is fully open at www.bklynlibrary.org/jsonapi — ~22 content types (node--branch, node--event, node--book_profile, node--digital_asset, node--eres, finding aids, and more) served as read-only JSON — real, comprehensive, and entirely UNDOCUMENTED and unadvertised.

Branch data on that API is rich: full address, weekly hours (seconds-from-midnight), geo point, subway/bus lines, holds-pickup flag, and ~40 service fields — far more than the two file-link datasets BPL posts to NYC Open Data.

What has NO owned public API is the catalog transaction layer, locked in the BiblioCommons ILS: searching the bibliographic catalog, the patron account/library card, and — above all — placing a HOLD on an item.

Net-new write surface: place a hold (BookHold) — the everyday library transaction, with no public API today; plus applying for a library card (a Drupal webform). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: FULLY PUBLIC, read-only. 289 resource links, ~22 node types (branch, event, external_event, book_profile, digital_asset, eres, exhibition, finding_aid, research_guide, podcasts, video, press_release, blog…). Real and comprehensive but undocumented/unadvertised — the accidental API. This is the content surface to formalize.

Bibliographic catalog search, patron account, library card, and holds/reservations. Server-rendered vendor app (SRV/app cookies, select_library redirect). The catalog write layer — placing a hold — has no owned public API.
The public programs/events calendar (bklynlibrary.org/calendar redirects here). Backs node--event; no documented JSON API of its own.
Only 2 BPL-labeled assets (BPL Branches, BPL Electronic Resources), both type=href external links — NOT live SODA tables. BPL, as a nonprofit, has no Open Data mandate.
server: cloudflare, cf-ray, cf-cache-status HIT on the main site and discover subdomain.

fruit.name: Library Branches
opendata_match: BPL Branches xmzf-uf2w (href, no SODA table)
fruit.notes: Core physical unit. JSON:API record carries full address, weekly hours, geo point, subway/bus, holds-pickup flag, and ~40 service fields. Keyed on Branch ID (field_branchid, e.g. '01' = Central Library).

fruit.name: Events & Programs
fruit.notes: Story times, classes, author talks. JSON:API record carries date range, program type, virtual/hybrid flags, offsite address, and a registration workflow (capacity, open/close, external URL). Program registration itself has no owned API.

fruit.name: Catalog (books & materials)
fruit.notes: The bibliographic catalog lives in the BiblioCommons ILS — no documented public API. The site's node--book_profile is only a curated editorial staff-pick twin, not the catalog record. A CatalogItem is what a BookHold targets.

fruit.name: Digital Collections
fruit.notes: Digitized assets, feature collections, and archival finding aids from the Center for Brooklyn History. JSON:API carries creator, place, alt title, audio derivative, note.

fruit.name: Electronic Resources
opendata_match: BPL Electronic Resources b7t4-zm44 (href, no SODA table)
fruit.notes: Licensed research databases available with a BPL card. JSON:API carries link, vendor, status, language, note, expiration.

fruit.name: Get a library card
fruit.notes: Applying for a card is a Drupal webform (webform_submission--library_card_access); reading a card (status/expiry/fines) lives in the BiblioCommons account. No owned public API. An alternative net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Place a hold (reservation)
fruit.notes: The single most common library transaction — reserve a catalog item and choose a pickup branch. Lives only inside the login-walled BiblioCommons catalog; no documented public API and no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.tagline: BPL already runs a full, public Drupal JSON:API it never advertised — formalize it, own it, and add the catalog hold write locked inside the BiblioCommons ILS.
meta.headline: The content API already exists and is public; the catalog transaction layer is locked in a vendor ILS.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern Drupal behind Cloudflare, but catalog/events on vendor systems (BiblioCommons, discover).
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A fully public, comprehensive Drupal JSON:API (~22 content types) already exists — best site API in the project, just undocumented.
scorecard_notes.openData: Only 2 BPL assets on NYC Open Data, both external file links, no SODA — nonprofit, no mandate.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Content data (branches/events/collections) rich and machine-readable; the catalog/bibliographic core is locked in BiblioCommons.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No public write anywhere — holds, card applications, and program registration are all vendor UI or webform.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP or agent-native surface today.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Owns the self-hosted Drupal JSON:API content; catalog, events, and card are vendor-owned.
